q:
The real weight of the car + the load + the persons; if you put everything on a certain moment on a scale, it is called …

A. ... the maximum authorized weight.
B. ... the maximum gross weight.
e:
LESSON 9 – ‘M.A.W. and M.G.W.’.
Maximum Gross weight M.G.W.
Read this lesson very well in your book.

q:
All documents that you show to the police must be original, except one document. Which one?

A. The registration certificate.
B. The insurance certificate.
C. The certificate of conformity.
e:
LESSON 9
All documents that must be carried in the vehicle have to be original.
The only exception is the insurance certificate: this document may also be shown digitally, for example as an image on your smartphone.

q:
Which of these signs relate to the Maximum Authorized Weight M.A.W. of a car?

A. 1 + 2.
B. 2 + 3 + 4.
C. 3 + 4.
e:
LESSON 9 – ‘M.A.W. and M.G.W.’.
Signs 1 relate to the M.G.W.
Since October 1, 2022, board three relates to the M.A.W.

LESSON 9 – ‘M.A.W. and M.G.W.’.
Signs 1 relate to the M.G.W.
Since October 1, 2022, board three relates to the M.A.W.

q:

A. Nr 1.
B. Nr 2.
C. Nr 3.
e:
LESSON 9 – ‘M.A.W. and M.G.W.’.
Reflective safety jacket – warning triangle – fire extinguisher – first aid kit.

LESSON 9 – ‘M.A.W. and M.G.W.’.
Signs 1, 2 and 3 (since 1/10/2022) relate to the M.A.W.

q:
What is car sharing?

A. With several people at the same time use the car to reach a destination together.
B. Occasionally using a car at times fixed in advance via reservation system.
e:
LESSON 9 – ‘M.A.W. and M.G.W.’.
If you share a car, you use one car with several people at a different time. You use the car when you need it. When you don't need it, someone else can drive it. In this way, a car is used better and thoughtfully and not everyone has to buy a car.

q: A car you can drive with a driving licence B is a car with a M.A.W. of maximum (…) kg.
e:
LESSON 9 – ‘M.A.W. and M.G.W.’.
Up to 3500 kg.
clean_q: A car you can drive with a driving licence B is a car with a M.A.W. of maximum (…) kg.
clean_e:
LESSON 9 – ‘M.A.W. and M.G.W.’.
Up to 3500 kg.

q:
You are in possession of a driving licence B. You drive in a car with a M.A.W. of 2500 kg.
How much is the maximum M.A.W. your trailer can have?

A. 500 kg.
B. 1000 kg.
C. 1500 kg.
e:
LESSON 9 – ‘M.A.W. and M.G.W.’.
As the M.A.W. of the trailer is more than 750 kg, a driving licence B shall be sufficient provided that the M. A.W. of the assembly (car + trailer) does not exceed 3500 kg.

clean_e:

As the M.A.W. of the trailer is more than 750 kg, a driving licence B shall be sufficient provided that the M. A.W. of the assembly (car + trailer) does not exceed 3500 kg.

q:
This combination of signs applies …

A. ... to the M.A.W.
B. ... to the M.G.W.
e:
LESSON 9 – ‘M.A.W. and M.G.W.’.
Starting from the road sign to the next intersection, it’s forbidden for drivers of vehicles with a M.A.W. of more than 3.5 ton to drive with greater speed than this that is referred to.

q:
A road with 2x2 lanes segregated by a verge.
Past these signs the blue car is allowed to drive maximum (…) kph.
e:
LESSON 9 – ‘M.A.W. and M.G.W.’.
Up to 90 km/hour for cars.
Vehicles with a M.A.W. of more than 3.5 ton: up to 70 km/hour.
Read this lesson very well in your book.

q:

A light truck is a vehicle with a M.A.W. up to 3500 kg.

q:
During a roadside check, the police ask for the vehicle registration certificate.
In which form must this document be presented?

A. The original.
B. The original or a colour copy.
C. A copy is sufficient, provided the original is presented later.
e:
LESSON 9
The following documents must be present in the vehicle:
- Registration certificate (original)
- Certificate of conformity (original)
- Insurance certificate (original, copy or digital version on a smartphone)
- Roadworthiness inspection certificate (original), once the vehicle is 4 years old
clean_q:

B. The original or a colour copy.
C. A copy is sufficient, provided the original is presented later.
clean_e:
LESSON 9
The following documents must be present in the vehicle:
- Registration certificate (original)
- Certificate of conformity (original)
- Insurance certificate (original, copy or digital version on a smartphone)
- Roadworthiness inspection certificate (original), once the vehicle is 4 years old

q:
The police ask for your driving licence during a check.

A. You must always carry the original driving licence with you.
B. You have 24 hours to present the original driving licence.
e:
LESSON 9
If you are driving, you must have your original driver's license with you.
